segments.text: Do not keep silent, O God of my praise!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful
Have opened against me; They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
segments.speaker: Enemies

segments.text: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, And fought against me without a cause.
In return for my love
they are my accusers, But I give myself to prayer.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Thus they have rewarded me evil for good, And hatred for my love.
Set a wicked man over him, And let an accuser stand at his right hand.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When he is judged, let him be found guilty, And let his prayer become sin.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let his days be few, And let another take his office.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let his children be fatherless, And his wife a widow.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let his children continually be vagabonds, and beg; Let them seek their bread also from their desolate places.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the creditor seize all that he has, And let strangers plunder his labor.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let there be none to extend mercy to him, Nor let there be any to favor his fatherless children.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let his posterity be cut off, And in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the Lord, And let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be continually before the Lord, That He may cut off the memory of them from the earth;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Because he did not remember to show mercy, But persecuted the poor and needy man, That he might even slay the broken in heart.
As he loved cursing, so let it come to him; As he did not delight in blessing, so let it be far from him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: As he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment, So let it enter his body like water, And like oil into his bones.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let it be to him like the garment which covers him, And for a belt with which he girds himself continually.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let this be the Lord's reward to my accusers, And to those who speak evil against my person.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But You, O God the Lord, Deal with me for Your name's sake; Because Your mercy is good, deliver me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For I am poor and needy, And my heart is wounded within me.
I am gone like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like a locust.
My knees are weak through fasting, And my flesh is feeble from lack of fatness.
I also have become a reproach to them; When they look at me, they shake their heads.
Help me, O Lord my God! Oh, save me according to Your mercy,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: That they may know that this is Your hand— That You, Lord, have done it!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them curse, but You bless; When they arise, let them be ashamed, But let Your servant rejoice.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let my accusers be clothed with shame, And let them cover themselves with their own disgrace as with a mantle.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will greatly praise the Lord with my mouth; Yes, I will praise Him among the multitude.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For He shall stand at the right hand of the poor, To save him from those who condemn him.
